titleStmt.title: SII 15.227: original edition by P. B. Desai, Dines Chandra Sircar and G. S. Gai
No. 227. (B.K. No. 454 of 1926.) VENKAṬĀPUR, DHARWAR TALUK, DHARWAR DISTRICT.

summary.p: On a slab set up in front of the temple.
The details of date preserved in this damaged and worn-out record are Śaka 105[8] and Uttarāyaṇa-saṁkrānti corresponding to 1136 A.D. December 25.

In the earlier portion of the epigraph, which appears to have contained a reference to the rule of a Chālukya suzerain, mention is made of a gift by Padmaladēvī. Subsequently it seems to record a gift made either by the Kadamba chief Jayakēśin II and his queen Maiḷala-dēvī or by some one at their instance. There is reference to rājadhāni Bhōgū[ru].

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
staṁbhāya śaṁbhavē Ōṁ nama śivāya
cakrava¨rtti vara vijaya
padmaladēvi biṭṭa dha¨rmma ©
svasti samadhigata paṁca samastabhuvana saṁstū
worn out
nijarājadhāni bhōgū
sakanṛpa kāḷātīta saṁvatsaraśataṁgaḷu 1058
vuttarāyaṇa saṁ
kēsidēvaru maiḷalamahādēviyaru kara
tami¨rddu ḍalvēḍi bi
yiṁ baḍaga tta 1
bāvi mataru drā¨rkkatāraṁbaraṁ salvaṁtā
gi bādheparihāravāgi dha¨rmmadi naḍasuvarellaru salisuvaru
Ī dha¨rmmamaṁ pāḷisidava¨rgge sāgaravāraṇāsi kuru kṣētra tī¨rtthasthānadalu sahasrakavile
yaṁ bahusuva¨rṇna garappa sabrāhmaṇa¨rgge [Ēkkōṭi] tapōdha na¨rgge koṭṭa phalamaneyduvaru
Idanaḷidāta vāraṇāsi kṣētradalu kavileya sabrāhmaṇarumaṁ divya tapōdhana
rumaṁ koṁda pātakanakku . bahubhi¨rvvasudhādattā rājabhissagarādibhiḥ yasyayasya yadā
bhuktā tasya tasya tadā phalaṁ [.] svadattāṁ paradattāṁ vā yōharēti vasundharāṁ ṣaṣṭhi ¨rvva¨rśa sa
hasrāṇi viṣṭhāyāṁ jāyatē krimiḥ . sāmānyōyaṁ dha¨rmmasētu nṛpāṇāṁ kā
lē kālē pālanīyō bhavadbhiḥ sa¨rvānētān bhāginaḥ pā¨rttivēṁdrān bhūyō bhūyō yā
catē rāmabhadraḥ . bīrabōva māḍisida dērummaṁ dēgulamumaṁ kāḷatraya dēvarāsi yā
cā¨ryya kesaṟu kallikki naṭṭa liṁga pratiṣṭha [Ī]du sāśvate bhukti .
Digital edition of SII 15.227 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
